query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity. Here's the summary:

**Latest picture (as of the July 9–10, 2026 data):** A tentative reversal after a record multi-month foreign selling wave.

**Cumulative selling:**
- H1 2026 (Jan–June): record **148.32 trillion won** (~$95B) net foreign selling
- YTD through July 3: **157.3 trillion won** (~$102.5B)
- ~90% concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix

**Recent weekly/monthly trend:**
- May–June alone: ~**92.89 trillion won** net sold
- Record single-day sale of **7.76 trillion won** (late June)
- **13 consecutive selling sessions** June 19–July 7

**Latest reversal:** Foreigners flipped to net buying July 8 (+343.6bn won) and July 9 (+134.8bn won) — first two-day buy streak since mid-June — while retail investors flipped to selling.

**Context:** This occurred despite a historic KOSPI rally (peaked 9,114.55 on June 22, up ~92–101% YTD), followed by sharp volatility and a drop to the ~7,200 support level (7,291.91 on July 9). Domestic individuals and institutions absorbed the outflows. Key drivers: profit-taking/rebalancing after the ~100% surge, won weakness, and semiconductor profit concerns.

The full report with all figures, sources, and structured data points has been delivered.

sources.title: Foreign investors' record Kospi sell-off may continue in second half as won weakens
sources.snippet: A record 148.32 trillion won ($95 billion) in net selling by foreign investors during the first half; record single-day net sale of 7.76 trillion won; 92.89 trillion won over May-June.

sources.title: Foreign investors may have more Korean stocks to sell
sources.snippet: Foreign investors sold 157.3 trillion won ($102.5 billion) on KOSPI YTD through July 3; index up 91.9%; ownership 40.47%; ~90% of selling in Samsung and SK Hynix.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Return, Fueling KOSPI Rebound Hopes
sources.snippet: Foreign net buy +343.6bn won (July 8) and +134.8bn won (July 9); first two-day buy streak since June 12-16; KOSPI closed 7,291.91 holding 7,200; June 22 peak 9,114.55.

sources.title: Kospi, SK Hynix, Samsung Electronics: Why foreign investors are selling
sources.snippet: Overseas investors unloaded a net 1.24 trillion won (~$801 million) of Kospi shares on June 8, 2026.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Dump 150 Trillion Won in KOSPI in First Half
sources.snippet: Foreign investors dumped nearly 150 trillion won of KOSPI shares in H1; KOSPI closed 8,476.48 on June 30.

sources.title: Foreigners Dump $106.8 Billion of Kospi Stocks in First Half as Samsung Ownership Hits 16-Year Low
sources.snippet: Foreign investors net sellers of 148.316 trillion won on Kospi in H1; selling concentrated in Samsung and SK Hynix; KOSPI +101.14%.

sources.title: Kospi selloff is rebalancing, not vote against South Korea: KRX CEO
sources.snippet: KRX CEO says selloff reflects portfolio rebalancing after Kospi grew 76% in 2025 and 108.85% YTD before June 3 selloff.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Absorb Retail Selloff as KOSPI Sees 1 Trillion Won Net Buying
sources.snippet: KOSPI reclaimed 8,500 with foreign investors net buying ~1 trillion won in mid-June.

key_facts: H1 2026 (Jan-June) foreign net selling on KOSPI: record 148.32 trillion won (~$95 billion)
YTD foreign net selling through July 3, 2026: 157.3 trillion won (~$102.5 billion)
~90% of H1 foreign selling concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix
May-June 2026 net foreign sales: ~92.89 trillion won over two months
Record single-day foreign net sale: 7.76 trillion won (late June 2026)
Foreigners were net sellers for 13 consecutive sessions from June 19 to July 7, 2026
Reversal: foreign net buy of +343.6 billion won (July 8) and +134.8 billion won (July 9) - first two-day net-buy streak since June 12-16
KOSPI peaked at 9,114.55 on June 22, 2026, up ~91.9-101% YTD, then fell sharply; closed 7,291.91 on July 9 holding 7,200 support
Domestic individuals bought ~99-105 trillion won and institutions ~35 trillion won, absorbing foreign outflows
Won depreciated from ~1,483 to ~1,549 per dollar over May-June; brokerages project 1,580/USD
Foreign ownership of KOSPI reported around 40.47% (early July), with drivers being rebalancing/profit-taking after ~100% rally and won weakness
